normalized_content: GitHub issue: [Bug]: OpenHands fails to start tasks — workspace directory cannot be created (`/workspace/project` exists even when it does not) URL: https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/issues/13109 Number: 13109 State: closed State reason: completed Created at: 2026-02-28T08:42:22Z Updated at: 2026-03-04T04:43:57Z Closed at: 2026-03-04T04:43:57Z Labels: bug Body: ### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? - [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate. ### Bug Description ### **Description** When running OpenHands via Docker Compose (OpenHands `1.4` with agent‑server `1.11.4-python`), tasks fail to start because the application cannot create the workspace directory inside the sandbox container. The error occurs even after deleting the entire `~/.openhands` directory on the host. The UI loads, skills load, conversations start, and the agent‑server connects normally. The failure happens only when a task is started. This issue occurs **even without GitHub integration**, so it is unrelated to provider configuration or DNS. --- ### **Error message** From container logs: ``` mkdir failed: mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/workspace/project’: File exists ``` This repeats every time a task is started. --- ### **Expected behavior** OpenHands should: - Create a clean workspace directory (`/workspace/project`) - Initialize the sandbox - Start the task normally --- ### **Actual behavior** - The workspace directory is reported as “already exists” - Tasks never start - No repo is cloned - No sandbox session is created This happens even when: - The host directory `~/.openhands/workspace/project` is deleted - The entire `~/.openhands` directory is deleted - The container is recreated - The image is pulled fresh - GitHub is not configured at all --- ### **Environment** **OpenHands version:** `docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:1.4` **Agent server:** `ghcr.io/openhands/agent-server:1.11.4-python` **Docker Compose snippet:** ```yaml version: '3.9' services: openhands: image: docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:1.4 container_name: openhands-app ports: - "3000:3000" volumes: - ~/.openhands:/.openhands - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock environment: LOG_ALL_EVENTS: "true" AGENT_SERVER_IMAGE_REPOSITORY: ghcr.io/openhands/agent-server AGENT_SERVER_IMAGE_TAG: 1.11.4-python extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" pull_policy: always tty: true stdin_open: true ``` **Host OS:** Debian 13 (fresh install) **Docker:** 24.x --- ### **Steps to reproduce** 1. Run OpenHands using the Compose file above 2. Open the UI at `http://localhost:3000` 3. Create a new conversation 4. Start any task (even a simple one like “create a file”) 5. Observe the logs --- ### **Relevant log excerpt** ``` 08:25:59 - openhands.app_server.app_conversation.app_conversation_service_base:WARNING: mkdir failed: mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/workspace/project’: File exists ``` This occurs even when the directory does not exist on the host. --- ### **Additional notes** - This issue appears to be a regression in the workspace initialization logic introduced in 1.4. - The sandbox runtime seems to believe the workspace exists even after a full reset. - The problem is reproducible on a clean system with no prior OpenHands state. --- ### **Impact** OpenHands cannot execute *any* tasks, making the application unusable in Docker deployments. --- If you'd like, I can also prepare a **minimal reproduction repo**, or a **shorter version** of the bug report if you prefer something more concise. ### Expected Behavior _No response_ ### Actual Behavior _No response_ ### Steps to Reproduce _No response_ ### OpenHands Installation Method CLI (uv tool install) ### If you selected "Other", please specify _No response_ ### OpenHands Version _No response_ ### Version Confirmation - [ ] I have confirmed this bug exists on the LATEST version of OpenHands ### Model Name _No response_ ### Operating System Linux ### Browser (if using web UI) chrome ### Logs and Error Messages 2026-Feb-28 08:35:22 INFO: 192.168.1.4:56630 -
